# Questions in Livestream 257

Live Stream #257: There Will Be A Test

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=939" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:39</a> | Can a wind setdown keep a body of water split apart for several hours? | Yes. He says wind setdown events can last that long and cites reports of much longer durations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=998" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:38</a> | Is 2D theatrical animation dead? | Yes. He says traditional theatrical 2D animation has largely been replaced by cheaper computer-assisted and 3D methods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=1117" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:37</a> | Is there anything a student can do to make a professor accept traditional Gospel authorship? | Not much. He says entrenched academic positions usually do not change because of one student's argument. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=1247" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:47</a> | What languages has Producer Falk studied? | He says her main language is English, with some French and some German. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=1478" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:38</a> | Should a writer limit how much pain a character must endure? | No. He says characters should be put under pressure because conflict and suffering drive story development. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=2295" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:15</a> | Does Hebrew have more than one script? | Yes. He distinguishes the modern square script from older Paleo-Hebrew and notes cursive forms as well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=3306" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:06</a> | What is happening in 1 Kings 13:4-5? | He says Jeroboam is acting as priest, is rebuked by God through the prophet, and receives a sign when his hand withers and the altar breaks. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=3455" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:35</a> | Why did the old prophet lie to the man of God in 1 Kings 13:18? | He says the text does not give a motive, but suggests the old prophet wanted the company of another true prophet and that the episode warns against trusting a prophet merely because he claims authority. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=4290" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:30</a> | In 1 Kings 14:25, do we have the Egyptian side of Shishak's campaign? | Yes in broad terms. He treats Shishak's campaign as historically recorded in Egyptian sources, though not with a biblical-style account of Jerusalem's plunder. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=5051" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:11</a> | Is there still a future for programmers and IT workers after the rise of AI? | Yes. He expects technical work to remain necessary even if AI changes the field. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=6547" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:07</a> | Does 1 Kings 16:34 imply Joshua was still in common memory? | Yes. He connects Hiel's rebuilding of Jericho with Joshua's curse and says the event would have reminded people of Joshua's pronouncement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=6782" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:02</a> | What is the purpose of the wind, earthquake, fire, and gentle blowing in 1 Kings 19:11-13? | He says the passage rejects the idea that God is simply in the dramatic elements and instead emphasizes God's presence in the still small voice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=8155" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:55</a> | Was Akhenaten monotheistic, henotheistic, or something else? | He says neither monotheist nor henotheist, but closer to a pantheist based on Aten theology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=9096" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:36</a> | What is the best way to answer a skeptic who says there is no evidence for biblical events like the Exodus or resurrection? | He separates the issues: resurrection evidence is largely testimonial, while he says the Exodus has substantial internal and historical evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=11212" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:52</a> | What do you think about Matthean priority versus Markan priority? | He leans toward Markan priority, while noting he does not have a strong stake in the debate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=11282" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:02</a> | Why is `fatness` translated as `anointing`? | He says the link is oil: anointing uses oil, and oil is understood as a kind of fat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=11368" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:28</a> | Could Aten theology have been influenced by Brahman in Hinduism? | He thinks that is unlikely because the evidence for Egypt-India contact is too thin and late to support a clear line of influence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=11486" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:11:26</a> | Does Jesus receiving sour wine in John 19:28-30 mean he completed the Passover cup? | He says no. He reads the hyssop and sour wine as evoking the Passover blood on the lintels rather than the fourth Passover cup. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=11832" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:12</a> | Would you recommend a master's degree in data science, AI, or cybersecurity? | He says cybersecurity is best learned on the job and would lean more toward a general computer science master's than a narrow specialization. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/pqkBVIijD7w?t=11893" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:13</a> | Could `Kasdim` be an ancient reference to the Kassites rather than later Chaldea? | He says `Kasdim` is the source behind Chaldea and Kassites, but cautions that the archaeological appearance of the Chaldeans is relatively late. |
